concerns.text: FR-002 restricts analysis to Python, Java, JavaScript, and TypeScript, explicitly excluding Go and Rust. The upstream idea specifies '50-100 open-source repositories' without language constraints, only requiring 'at least 2 years of commit history'. This arbitrary language exclusion reduces the potential sample size and introduces selection bias not requested by the idea, potentially under-representing modern high-churn ecosystems.

FR-007 mandates excluding files with <10 lines of code. The upstream idea does not specify a minimum file size threshold. While this may be a methodological choice, it is an unrequested constraint that alters the dataset composition. If the idea intended to capture 'all files', this exclusion is an over-reach that needs justification or removal to match the original scope.

FR-006 requires Bonferroni correction for >10 repositories. The idea mentions 'Results will be validated across multiple projects' but does not specify a statistical correction method or a threshold (10 repos) for triggering it. While statistical rigor is often implied, explicitly hard-coding a specific correction method and trigger threshold in the spec without it being in the idea or a prior methodology panel decision constitutes scope addition (over-reach).
